settings.heading: Clear, Healthy Nails Start From Within
settings.subtext: Dr. Langford's clinically-studied antifungal serum penetrates through the nail bed to fight fungus at its source — naturally, safely, and effectively.
settings.disclaimer: ✓ Free Shipping  ✓ 90-Day Guarantee  ✓ Made in USA

settings.review_text: I've tried everything — pills, creams, even vinegar soaks. After 6 weeks of Keraclear, I could see new clear nail growth. Month 3 now and my nails look better than they have in years.
I was embarrassed to wear sandals for 5 years. Keraclear was the perfect alternative — natural ingredients, easy to apply, and it actually delivered results.

settings.review_title: The best nail treatment I've used
settings.review_text: As a nurse on my feet all day, I was skeptical. But Keraclear's oil formula absorbs so much better than creams. After 8 weeks, the discoloration is almost completely gone.

settings.title: Surface treatments can't reach the root
settings.content: Topical creams and polishes sit on top of the nail. They never penetrate to where fungus actually lives — beneath the nail bed, protected by a biofilm shield.

settings.title: Oral medications come with serious side effects
settings.content: Prescription antifungals like terbinafine require liver function monitoring and can cause nausea, headaches, and liver damage. Many doctors won't prescribe them for mild cases.

settings.title: The biofilm barrier blocks everything
settings.content: Nail fungus creates a protective biofilm — a microscopic shield that blocks 99% of treatments. Without breaking through this barrier first, nothing can reach the fungus.

settings.title: Most people quit too early
settings.content: Visible nail growth takes 3-6 months. Many treatments show no visible progress for weeks, causing people to give up before the treatment has time to work.

settings.credentials: Harvard Medical School Graduate • 22 Years in Dermatology
settings.quote: After 22 years treating nail fungus, I developed KeraClear because I was frustrated watching my patients fail with the same ineffective treatments. The key breakthrough was our biofilm penetration technology — once you break through that barrier, the fungus has nowhere to hide.
